ext_description.message: Browser proxy manager - enhanced: click to toggle, right-click for settings

mode_desc_direct.message: Bypass all proxies and connect directly. Takes priority over the system proxy—if a tool like Clash Verge sets a system proxy, Chrome still connects directly. However, TUN mode (virtual NIC) operates at the network layer and cannot be bypassed.

mode_desc_fixed.message: Route traffic through a fixed proxy server you specify. Supports HTTP/HTTPS/SOCKS4/SOCKS5 and per-protocol rules. Best when you know the exact proxy address.

mode_desc_auto.message: Discovers a PAC (Proxy Auto-Config) script on the network via WPAD; the browser then routes each request based on the script's return value. Requires WPAD/PAC to be deployed on the network. Common in corporate or school networks.

mode_desc_system.message: Use the operating system's proxy settings. The browser follows the Windows/macOS system proxy configuration. Ideal when a global proxy is already set at the OS level.

hint.message: Tip: click the icon to toggle between Fixed Servers and System

hint_first_use.message: First-time setup: enter your proxy host and port, then save. After that, click the icon to toggle the proxy quickly.

alert_port_range.message: Port must be a number between 1 and 65535

alert_rules.message: Please select at least one proxy rule
